exemplar.title: To what extent did KFCâs (United States) diversification through the introduction to plant-based chicken in partnership with Beyond Meat influence its Market Share and Market Position in the fast-food industry in 2020?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did KFCâs (United States) diversification through the introduction to plant-based chicken in partnership with Beyond Meat influence its Market Share and Market Position in the fast-food industry in 2020?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The research question is precise, clearly stated on the title page, and remains central throughout the report.
Findings are consistently linked back to the investigationâs focus on KFCâs market share and position, demonstrating effective communication.
A suitable range of relevant secondary sources and analytical tools is identified and applied.
weaknesses: Some generic descriptive passages dilute the tight focus of the investigation.
Methodology relies heavily on secondary sources (e.g., Wikipedia, YouTube) without primary data or triangulation.
The list of analytical tools is incomplete in places, reducing methodological clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on strand assessments, the topic is communicated accurately and the research question is well-focused and effectively connected (band 5). Method selection is appropriate though not exemplary, showing adequate evidence of informed choice (band 4). Overall, performance aligns with the upper end of the 5-6 band but minor lapses prevent full marks, justifying a score of 5.

strengths: Source material (e.g., Statista, Nielsen, academic papers) is mostly relevant and integrated into narrative and diagrams.
The student demonstrates clear understanding of key business concepts and models (Ansoff, Brand Prism, perceptual mapping).
Terminology (e.g., market share, CAGR, brand equity) is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Several statistics and quotations lack formal citations, undermining credibility.
Reliance on non-peer sources (Wikipedia, YouTube) raises concerns about reliability.
Some factual claims (e.g., outlet counts, sales growth) are presented without reference.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of source material is mostly relevant but inconsistent, placing performance in the 3-4 band. Subject knowledge is clear and coherent with effective concept use (band 4), and terminology is accurate (band 4). Minor citation and source-quality issues prevent a top-band score, warranting a 4.

strengths: Research demonstrates good breadth, covering market size data, brand-value trends, competitor products and consumer surveys, all relevant to the research question.
The analysis clearly links the COVID-19 context to KFCâs diversification strategy, showing strong critical insight.
Arguments about brand value impact and competitive positioning are coherent and well-developed.
weaknesses: Numerical analysis is limited, with few precise data points or tables to support claims.
Evaluation of sources and methods is superficial, lacking deep critique of limitations.
Discussion would benefit from more sophisticated evidence (e.g., specific share figures) to ground arguments.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate delivers good, consistently relevant research (band 7-9), clear analysis with supported conclusions (band 7-9), and a well-developed argument with partially critical evaluation (band 7-9). The workâs strengths in breadth and insight place it at the top of this band, meriting a score of 9 in Critical Thinking.

strengths: The report structure follows EE conventions (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography).
Headings, subheadings, and a table of contents aid navigation and demonstrate logical organization.
Layout elements such as word count, title, and embedded visuals are correctly applied.
weaknesses: Figure captions and numbering are inconsistent or lacking detail.
Contents page lacks uniform dot-leader formatting between headings and page numbers.
Citation formatting is uneven and page numbering is not consistently visible.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and layout meet the clear requirements of the top band (band 3-4), but minor formatting inconsistencies and incomplete captions prevent full marks. A score of 3 reflects solid presentation with room for polish.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly reproduced on the title page, remains central throughout all sections, and is narrow (U.S. KFC, 2020).  The discussion sections (Ansoff, Brand Prism, perception map, statistics) repeatedly relate findings back to market share/position, showing effective connection.  Focus is marginally diluted by some generic description, hence a solid 5 rather than 6.

The student presents a coherent argument that the strategy improved brand value and competitive position, referencing both qualitative and quantitative indicators.  Evaluation acknowledges limitations (lack of primary data) but critique of sources, methods and alternative strategies is quite superficial.  This fits the âwell-developed but partially criticalâ descriptor.

The essay shows clear understanding of Ansoff Matrix, Brand Identity Prism and perceptual mapping, applying them correctly to KFC.  Explanations of diversification risk, brand personality shifts and competitive positioning are coherent.  Depth is satisfactory though not especially sophisticated, warranting the upper end of the adequate band.

Business-management terminology (diversification, market share, CAGR, brand equity, QSR) is used accurately and consistently.  Occasional spelling/formatting issues and a few mis-phrased sentences prevent a flawless performance but the language shows mostly correct conceptual use.

Frameworks are applied to derive conclusions (e.g., diversification attracted health-conscious consumers).  Yet numerical analysis is thin (single bar chart, no actual share figures) and some claims are asserted rather than evidenced.  Analysis is clear but only moderately supported, meriting a low-mid score in this band.

Source material (e.g., Statista brand-value chart, PBFA survey, Nielsen data) is integrated into narrative and diagrams, demonstrating relevance.  However, quotations and statistics are often used descriptively with little critical handling, and some figures (sell-out times) remain unreferenced, so application is mostly but not consistently appropriate.

A reasonable variety of secondary sources (market reports, Statista, Forbes, academic papers) and four analytical tools are identified.  Source selection is partly informed, yet reliance on Wikipedia/YouTube and the absence of any primary data or triangulation limit methodological robustness.  Coverage is therefore adequate rather than exemplary.

Research covers market size data, brand-value trends, competitor products and consumer surveys, all clearly relevant to the question.  The breadth is good, though depth of primary evidence is missing, keeping it in the middle of the âgoodâ band.

Word count, title and candidate details are provided; headings and sub-headings are used; visuals are embedded.  Page numbering is not visible in the extract and citation formatting is inconsistent, yet the majority of layout conventions are met correctly, justifying a solid score within the top band.

The student states a precise title and a clearly bounded research question that specifies firm, market, product, partnership and single-year focus. Background paragraphs and the rationale explain why the topic matters (shifts in consumer preferences, pandemic context) and how it links to business theory. Explanations are detailed and coherent, so the reader fully understands the investigative focus. Minor lapses in phrasing do not materially hinder comprehension.

The report follows the prescribed EE structure with title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion and bibliography.  Minor omissions (in-text figure numbering inconsistencies, some clutter in tables) prevent full marks but overall structure is clearly appropriate.

annotations.comment: The CFO quote adds authoritative voice but lacks a formal citation; include page or URL reference for verification.
The table of contents lists all required sections and shows logical organization, aiding navigation and demonstrating adherence to structure conventions.
The historical expansion statistics (24,000 outlets, 140 markets) are presented without a source citation; ensure all factual claims are properly referenced.
The research question is exceptionally precise and remains central throughout, establishing a clear investigative focus on KFCâs diversification and its impact on market share in 2020.
The word count is shown and stays just under the 4000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirement and reflecting careful scope management.
Figure on the title page lacks a descriptive caption explaining its purpose; add a concise legend to clarify how it frames the investigation.
Entries in the contents lack consistent dot-leader formatting between headings and page numbers; consider applying uniform leader lines for clarity.
The discussion of market growth in 2020 is insightful but lacks citation; integrate a relevant Statista or industry report reference.
The phrase âameliorated with a trending pressure frying methodâ is awkward; use precise business terminology to describe operational innovation more clearly.
Reliance on Wikipedia for foundational branding discussion raises credibility concerns; incorporate peer-reviewed or industry-standard sources instead.
This descriptive account of KFCâs synergy is well-written but could be strengthened by linking explicitly back to the research question.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent (raw URLs, HTML entities); adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) for professionalism.
The 45% sales growth claim is compelling but presented without in-text citation; add parenthetical citation to bolster credibility.
Footnote content includes stray HTML entities and inconsistent superscript syntax; standardize formatting to improve readability.
Presenting raw market value and CAGR is helpful; consider showing a small table of annual values for more precise trend analysis.
Linking the pandemic context to diversification rationale demonstrates strong critical insight into external factors shaping strategy.
Discussion of the Ansoff matrix is thorough conceptually but would benefit from a specific data point (e.g., diversification share) to ground the analysis.
The rationale for combining nutrition, sustainability, taste, and ethics is logically articulated but would benefit from linking each factor to specific consumer data.
The description of the KFCâBeyond Meat pilot tests is thorough but contains malformed math tags; correct LaTeX or remove stray markup.
The rationale paragraph effectively restates the research purpose but is overly long; break into bullet points or sub-sections to enhance readability.
Description of resource limitations remains generic; specify particular gaps (e.g., absence of primary survey, real-time sales data) for depth.
The analytical tools list stops mid-sentence; ensure each toolâs purpose and method are fully described for methodological rigor.
âAnalysis & Discussionâ header is prominently formatted, guiding the reader effectively into the core analytical chapters.
Multiple footnotes reference YouTube and non-peer sources without clear differentiation of reliability; prioritize scholarly or industry reports where possible.
Methodology section is clearly labeled and follows EE conventions, supporting easy navigation and logical flow.
Ansoff matrix figure lacks detailed quadrant labels and legend; annotate axes and risk-reward dimensions for reader comprehension.
